authors_text: Neelay Joglekar,Fei Liu,Ryan Orosco,Michael Yip
title: Suture Thread Spline Reconstruction from Endoscopic Images for Robotic Surgery with Reliability-driven Keypoint Detection
title_zh: 通过可靠性驱动的关键点检测从内窥镜图像重建机器人手术的缝合线样条
abstract: Automating the process of manipulating and delivering sutures during robotic surgery is a prominent problem at the frontier of surgical robotics, as automating this task can significantly reduce surgeons' fatigue during tele-operated surgery and allow them to spend more time addressing higher-level clinical decision making. Accomplishing autonomous suturing and suture manipulation in the real world requires accurate suture thread localization and reconstruction, the process of creating a 3D shape representation of suture thread from 2D stereo camera surgical image pairs. This is a very challenging problem due to how limited pixel information is available for the threads, as well as their sensitivity to lighting and specular reflection. We present a suture thread reconstruction work that uses reliable keypoints and a Minimum Variation Spline (MVS) smoothing optimization to construct a 3D centerline from a segmented surgical image pair. This method is comparable to previous suture thread reconstruction works, with the possible benefit of increased accuracy of grasping point estimation. Our code and datasets will be available at: https://github.com/ucsdarclab/thread-reconstruction.
summary: Automating the process of manipulating and delivering sutures during robotic surgery is a prominent problem at the frontier of surgical robotics, as automating this task can significantly reduce surgeons' fatigue during tele-operated surgery and allow them to spend more time addressing higher-level clinical decision making. Accomplishing autonomous suturing and suture manipulation in the real world requires accurate suture thread localization and reconstruction, the process of creating a 3D shape representation of suture thread from 2D stereo camera surgical image pairs. This is a very challenging problem due to how limited pixel information is available for the threads, as well as their sensitivity to lighting and specular reflection. We present a suture thread reconstruction work that uses reliable keypoints and a Minimum Variation Spline (MVS) smoothing optimization to construct a 3D centerline from a segmented surgical image pair. This method is comparable to previous suture thread reconstruction works, with the possible benefit of increased accuracy of grasping point estimation. Our code and datasets will be available at: https://github.com/ucsdarclab/thread-reconstruction.
abstract_zh: 机器人手术过程中操纵和输送缝线过程的自动化是手术机器人技术前沿的一个突出问题，因为自动化这项任务可以显着减少外科医生在远程手术过程中的疲劳，让他们有更多的时间来处理更高级别的临床决策。在现实世界中实现自主缝合和缝合操作需要精确的缝合线定位和重建，即从 2D 立体相机手术图像对创建缝合线的 3D 形状表示的过程。这是一个非常具有挑战性的问题，因为线程可用的像素信息非常有限，而且它们对光照和镜面反射的敏感性。我们提出了一种缝合线重建工作，该工作使用可靠的关键点和最小变化样条 (MVS) 平滑优化来从分割的手术图像对构建 3D 中心线。该方法与以前的缝合线重建工作相当，可能具有提高抓取点估计准确性的好处。我们的代码和数据集将在以下位置提供：https://github.com/ucsdarclab/thread-reconstruction。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种从内窥镜图像中重建缝合线三维结构的方法，用于提升机器人手术的自动化水平。
AI.motivation: 缝合线的自动操控可减轻外科医生负担，但现有方法因缝合线像素信息有限及光照干扰而难以准确重建其三维形态。
AI.method: 结合可靠性关键点检测与最小方差样条优化算法，从分割后的立体图像对中生成缝合线三维中心线。
AI.result: 该方法在重建精度上与现有技术相当，且可能提升抓取点估计的准确性，代码与数据集已开源。
AI.conclusion: 提出的方法为手术机器人自主缝合提供了可行方案，未来可通过优化关键点检测进一步提升性能。

result: 该方法在重建精度上与现有技术相当，且可能提升抓取点估计的准确性，代码与数据集已开源。
conclusion: 提出的方法为手术机器人自主缝合提供了可行方案，未来可通过优化关键点检测进一步提升性能。
search_text: Suture Thread Spline Reconstruction from Endoscopic Images for Robotic Surgery with Reliability-driven Keypoint Detection 通过可靠性驱动的关键点检测从内窥镜图像重建机器人手术的缝合线样条 Automating the process of manipulating and delivering sutures during robotic surgery is a prominent problem at the frontier of surgical robotics, as automating this task can significantly reduce surgeons' fatigue during tele-operated surgery and allow them to spend more time addressing higher-level clinical decision making. Accomplishing autonomous suturing and suture manipulation in the real world requires accurate suture thread localization and reconstruction, the process of creating a 3D shape representation of suture thread from 2D stereo camera surgical image pairs. This is a very challenging problem due to how limited pixel information is available for the threads, as well as their sensitivity to lighting and specular reflection. We present a suture thread reconstruction work that uses reliable keypoints and a Minimum Variation Spline (MVS) smoothing optimization to construct a 3D centerline from a segmented surgical image pair. This method is comparable to previous suture thread reconstruction works, with the possible benefit of increased accuracy of grasping point estimation. Our code and datasets will be available at: https://github.com/ucsdarclab/thread-reconstruction. 机器人手术过程中操纵和输送缝线过程的自动化是手术机器人技术前沿的一个突出问题，因为自动化这项任务可以显着减少外科医生在远程手术过程中的疲劳，让他们有更多的时间来处理更高级别的临床决策。在现实世界中实现自主缝合和缝合操作需要精确的缝合线定位和重建，即从 2D 立体相机手术图像对创建缝合线的 3D 形状表示的过程。这是一个非常具有挑战性的问题，因为线程可用的像素信息非常有限，而且它们对光照和镜面反射的敏感性。我们提出了一种缝合线重建工作，该工作使用可靠的关键点和最小变化样条 (MVS) 平滑优化来从分割的手术图像对构建 3D 中心线。该方法与以前的缝合线重建工作相当，可能具有提高抓取点估计准确性的好处。我们的代码和数据集将在以下位置提供：https://github.com/ucsdarclab/thread-reconstruction。 本文提出了一种从内窥镜图像中重建缝合线三维结构的方法，用于提升机器人手术的自动化水平。 Neelay Joglekar,Fei Liu,Ryan Orosco,Michael Yip cs.RO

authors_text: Jin-Soo Park,Xuesu Xiao,Garrett Warnell,Harel Yedidsion,Peter Stone
title: Learning Perceptual Hallucination for Multi-Robot Navigation in Narrow Hallways
title_zh: 学习狭窄走廊中多机器人导航的知觉幻觉
abstract: While current systems for autonomous robot navigation can produce safe and efficient motion plans in static environments, they usually generate suboptimal behaviors when multiple robots must navigate together in confined spaces. For example, when two robots meet each other in a narrow hallway, they may either turn around to find an alternative route or collide with each other. This paper presents a new approach to navigation that allows two robots to pass each other in a narrow hallway without colliding, stopping, or waiting. Our approach, Perceptual Hallucination for Hallway Passing (PHHP), learns to synthetically generate virtual obstacles (i.e., perceptual hallucination) to facilitate passing in narrow hallways by multiple robots that utilize otherwise standard autonomous navigation systems. Our experiments on physical robots in a variety of hallways show improved performance compared to multiple baselines.
summary: While current systems for autonomous robot navigation can produce safe and efficient motion plans in static environments, they usually generate suboptimal behaviors when multiple robots must navigate together in confined spaces. For example, when two robots meet each other in a narrow hallway, they may either turn around to find an alternative route or collide with each other. This paper presents a new approach to navigation that allows two robots to pass each other in a narrow hallway without colliding, stopping, or waiting. Our approach, Perceptual Hallucination for Hallway Passing (PHHP), learns to synthetically generate virtual obstacles (i.e., perceptual hallucination) to facilitate passing in narrow hallways by multiple robots that utilize otherwise standard autonomous navigation systems. Our experiments on physical robots in a variety of hallways show improved performance compared to multiple baselines.
abstract_zh: 虽然当前的自主机器人导航系统可以在静态环境中生成安全高效的运动计划，但当多个机器人必须在有限空间中一起导航时，它们通常会产生次优的行为。例如，当两个机器人在狭窄的走廊中相遇时，它们可能会转身寻找替代路线或相互碰撞。本文提出了一种新的导航方法，允许两个机器人在狭窄的走廊中相互通过，而不会发生碰撞、停止或等待。我们的方法，走廊通过的感知幻觉（PHHP），学习综合生成虚拟障碍（即感知幻觉），以方便使用其他标准自主导航系统的多个机器人在狭窄的走廊中通过。我们在各种走廊中对物理机器人进行的实验表明，与多个基线相比，性能有所提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为PHHP的方法，通过生成虚拟障碍物使多机器人在狭窄走廊中安全通过，避免碰撞和等待。
AI.motivation: 现有自主导航系统在静态环境表现良好，但多机器人协作时在狭窄空间易出现碰撞或效率低下的问题。
AI.method: PHHP通过学习生成感知幻觉（虚拟障碍物），引导机器人使用标准导航系统实现协同通过。
AI.result: 在多种物理机器人实验中，PHHP的表现优于多个基线方法，有效提升通过效率与安全性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过感知幻觉机制成功解决了多机器人狭窄空间导航的挑战，为协作导航提供了新思路。

authors_text: Xingping Dong,Jianbing Shen,Ling Shao
title: Rethinking Clustering-Based Pseudo-Labeling for Unsupervised Meta-Learning
title_zh: 重新思考无监督元学习中基于聚类的伪标签
comment: Accepted by European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
comments: Accepted by European Conference on Computer Vision (ECCV), 2022
abstract: The pioneering method for unsupervised meta-learning, CACTUs, is a clustering-based approach with pseudo-labeling. This approach is model-agnostic and can be combined with supervised algorithms to learn from unlabeled data. However, it often suffers from label inconsistency or limited diversity, which leads to poor performance. In this work, we prove that the core reason for this is lack of a clustering-friendly property in the embedding space. We address this by minimizing the inter- to intra-class similarity ratio to provide clustering-friendly embedding features, and validate our approach through comprehensive experiments. Note that, despite only utilizing a simple clustering algorithm (k-means) in our embedding space to obtain the pseudo-labels, we achieve significant improvement. Moreover, we adopt a progressive evaluation mechanism to obtain more diverse samples in order to further alleviate the limited diversity problem. Finally, our approach is also model-agnostic and can easily be integrated into existing supervised methods. To demonstrate its generalization ability, we integrate it into two representative algorithms: MAML and EP. The results on three main few-shot benchmarks clearly show that the proposed method achieves significant improvement compared to state-of-the-art models. Notably, our approach also outperforms the corresponding supervised method in two tasks.
summary: The pioneering method for unsupervised meta-learning, CACTUs, is a clustering-based approach with pseudo-labeling. This approach is model-agnostic and can be combined with supervised algorithms to learn from unlabeled data. However, it often suffers from label inconsistency or limited diversity, which leads to poor performance. In this work, we prove that the core reason for this is lack of a clustering-friendly property in the embedding space. We address this by minimizing the inter- to intra-class similarity ratio to provide clustering-friendly embedding features, and validate our approach through comprehensive experiments. Note that, despite only utilizing a simple clustering algorithm (k-means) in our embedding space to obtain the pseudo-labels, we achieve significant improvement. Moreover, we adopt a progressive evaluation mechanism to obtain more diverse samples in order to further alleviate the limited diversity problem. Finally, our approach is also model-agnostic and can easily be integrated into existing supervised methods. To demonstrate its generalization ability, we integrate it into two representative algorithms: MAML and EP. The results on three main few-shot benchmarks clearly show that the proposed method achieves significant improvement compared to state-of-the-art models. Notably, our approach also outperforms the corresponding supervised method in two tasks.
abstract_zh: 无监督元学习的开创性方法 CACTU 是一种基于聚类的伪标记方法。这种方法与模型无关，可以与监督算法相结合，从未标记的数据中学习。然而，它经常遭受标签不一致或多样性有限的问题，从而导致性能不佳。在这项工作中，我们证明了其核心原因是嵌入空间中缺乏聚类友好的属性。我们通过最小化类间与类内相似度来解决这个问题，以提供聚类友好的嵌入特征，并通过综合实验验证我们的方法。请注意，尽管在我们的嵌入空间中仅使用简单的聚类算法（k-means）来获取伪标签，但我们取得了显着的改进。此外，我们采用渐进式评估机制来获得更多样的样本，以进一步缓解多样性有限的问题。最后，我们的方法也是与模型无关的，并且可以轻松集成到现有的监督方法中。为了展示其泛化能力，我们将其集成到两个代表性算法中：MAML 和 EP。三个主要的几次基准测试的结果清楚地表明，与最先进的模型相比，所提出的方法取得了显着的改进。值得注意的是，我们的方法在两项任务中也优于相应的监督方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种改进的基于聚类的伪标签方法，用于解决无监督元学习中的标签不一致和多样性不足问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法如CACTUs在无监督元学习中因嵌入空间缺乏聚类友好性，导致伪标签质量差，性能受限。
AI.method: 通过最小化类间与类内相似度比值生成聚类友好嵌入，结合k-means生成伪标签，并引入渐进式评估机制提升样本多样性。
AI.result: 在三个主流少样本基准测试中显著优于现有模型，且在两个任务中超越对应监督方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了嵌入空间聚类不友好问题，具有模型无关性且易于集成到现有监督算法中。

authors_text: Apan Dastider,Hao Fang,Mingjie Lin
title: Unified Control Framework for Real-Time Interception and Obstacle Avoidance of Fast-Moving Objects with Diffusion Variational Autoencoder
title_zh: 扩散变分自编码器实时拦截和快速移动物体避障的统一控制框架
comment: Submitted to IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS) 2024
comments: Submitted to IEEE International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS) 2024
abstract: Real-time interception of fast-moving objects by robotic arms in dynamic environments poses a formidable challenge due to the need for rapid reaction times, often within milliseconds, amidst dynamic obstacles. This paper introduces a unified control framework to address the above challenge by simultaneously intercepting dynamic objects and avoiding moving obstacles. Central to our approach is using diffusion-based variational autoencoder for motion planning to perform both object interception and obstacle avoidance. We begin by encoding the high-dimensional temporal information from streaming events into a two-dimensional latent manifold, enabling the discrimination between safe and colliding trajectories, culminating in the construction of an offline densely connected trajectory graph. Subsequently, we employ an extended Kalman filter to achieve precise real-time tracking of the moving object. Leveraging a graph-traversing strategy on the established offline dense graph, we generate encoded robotic motor control commands. Finally, we decode these commands to enable real-time motion of robotic motors, ensuring effective obstacle avoidance and high interception accuracy of fast-moving objects. Experimental validation on both computer simulations and autonomous 7-DoF robotic arms demonstrates the efficacy of our proposed framework. Results indicate the capability of the robotic manipulator to navigate around multiple obstacles of varying sizes and shapes while successfully intercepting fast-moving objects thrown from different angles by hand. Complete video demonstrations of our experiments can be found in https://sites.google.com/view/multirobotskill/home.

Results indicate the capability of the robotic manipulator to navigate around multiple obstacles of varying sizes and shapes while successfully intercepting fast-moving objects thrown from different angles by hand. Complete video demonstrations of our experiments can be found in https://sites.google.com/view/multirobotskill/home.
abstract_zh: 由于在动态障碍物中需要快速反应时间（通常在几毫秒内），因此在动态环境中通过机械臂实时拦截快速移动的物体构成了巨大的挑战。本文介绍了一种统一的控制框架，通过同时拦截动态物体并避开移动障碍物来解决上述挑战。我们方法的核心是使用基于扩散的变分自动编码器进行运动规划，以执行对象拦截和避障。我们首先将流事件中的高维时间信息编码为二维潜在流形，从而能够区分安全轨迹和碰撞轨迹，最终构建离线密集连接的轨迹图。随后，我们采用扩展卡尔曼滤波器来实现对运动物体的精确实时跟踪。利用已建立的离线密集图的图遍历策略，我们生成编码的机器人电机控制命令。最后，我们对这些命令进行解码，以实现机器人电机的实时运动，确保有效避障和对快速移动物体的高拦截精度。对计算机模拟和自主 7 自由度机械臂的实验验证证明了我们提出的框架的有效性。结果表明，机器人操纵器能够绕过不同大小和形状的多个障碍物，同时成功拦截用手从不同角度抛出的快速移动物体。我们实验的完整视频演示可以在 https://sites.google.com/view/multirobotskill/home 中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于扩散变分自编码器的统一控制框架，用于实现实时拦截快速移动物体并规避动态障碍物。
AI.motivation: 在动态环境中，机械臂需在毫秒级时间内同时完成快速移动物体的拦截与动态障碍物规避，传统方法难以兼顾效率与安全性。
AI.method: 通过扩散变分自编码器将高维时序数据编码为二维潜在流形，构建离线密集轨迹图；结合扩展卡尔曼滤波实时跟踪目标，并利用图遍历策略生成控制指令，最终解码驱动机械臂运动。
AI.result: 实验验证显示，该框架在仿真与真实7-DoF机械臂上均能有效规避多形态障碍物，成功拦截从不同角度抛掷的快速移动物体，视频演示见论文链接。
AI.conclusion: 所提框架通过潜在空间轨迹建模与实时控制解码，实现了复杂动态场景下机械臂的高精度拦截与避障，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: Unified Control Framework for Real-Time Interception and Obstacle Avoidance of Fast-Moving Objects with Diffusion Variational Autoencoder 扩散变分自编码器实时拦截和快速移动物体避障的统一控制框架 Real-time interception of fast-moving objects by robotic arms in dynamic environments poses a formidable challenge due to the need for rapid reaction times, often within milliseconds, amidst dynamic obstacles. This paper introduces a unified control framework to address the above challenge by simultaneously intercepting dynamic objects and avoiding moving obstacles. Central to our approach is using diffusion-based variational autoencoder for motion planning to perform both object interception and obstacle avoidance. We begin by encoding the high-dimensional temporal information from streaming events into a two-dimensional latent manifold, enabling the discrimination between safe and colliding trajectories, culminating in the construction of an offline densely connected trajectory graph. Subsequently, we employ an extended Kalman filter to achieve precise real-time tracking of the moving object. Leveraging a graph-traversing strategy on the established offline dense graph, we generate encoded robotic motor control commands. Finally, we decode these commands to enable real-time motion of robotic motors, ensuring effective obstacle avoidance and high interception accuracy of fast-moving objects. Experimental validation on both computer simulations and autonomous 7-DoF robotic arms demonstrates the efficacy of our proposed framework. Results indicate the capability of the robotic manipulator to navigate around multiple obstacles of varying sizes and shapes while successfully intercepting fast-moving objects thrown from different angles by hand. Complete video demonstrations of our experiments can be found in https://sites.google.com/view/multirobotskill/home. 由于在动态障碍物中需要快速反应时间（通常在几毫秒内），因此在动态环境中通过机械臂实时拦截快速移动的物体构成了巨大的挑战。本文介绍了一种统一的控制框架，通过同时拦截动态物体并避开移动障碍物来解决上述挑战。我们方法的核心是使用基于扩散的变分自动编码器进行运动规划，以执行对象拦截和避障。我们首先将流事件中的高维时间信息编码为二维潜在流形，从而能够区分安全轨迹和碰撞轨迹，最终构建离线密集连接的轨迹图。随后，我们采用扩展卡尔曼滤波器来实现对运动物体的精确实时跟踪。利用已建立的离线密集图的图遍历策略，我们生成编码的机器人电机控制命令。最后，我们对这些命令进行解码，以实现机器人电机的实时运动，确保有效避障和对快速移动物体的高拦截精度。对计算机模拟和自主 7 自由度机械臂的实验验证证明了我们提出的框架的有效性。结果表明，机器人操纵器能够绕过不同大小和形状的多个障碍物，同时成功拦截用手从不同角度抛出的快速移动物体。我们实验的完整视频演示可以在 https://sites.google.com/view/multirobotskill/home 中找到。 本文提出一种基于扩散变分自编码器的统一控制框架，用于实现实时拦截快速移动物体并规避动态障碍物。 Apan Dastider,Hao Fang,Mingjie Lin cs.RO,cs.AI

authors_text: Kaiping Chen,Anqi Shao,Jirayu Burapacheep,Yixuan Li
title: How GPT-3 responds to different publics on climate change and Black Lives Matter: A critical appraisal of equity in conversational AI
title_zh: GPT-3 如何回应不同公众关于气候变化和“黑人生命也是命”的问题：对话式人工智能公平性的批判性评估
abstract: Autoregressive language models, which use deep learning to produce human-like texts, have become increasingly widespread. Such models are powering popular virtual assistants in areas like smart health, finance, and autonomous driving. While the parameters of these large language models are improving, concerns persist that these models might not work equally for all subgroups in society. Despite growing discussions of AI fairness across disciplines, there lacks systemic metrics to assess what equity means in dialogue systems and how to engage different populations in the assessment loop. Grounded in theories of deliberative democracy and science and technology studies, this paper proposes an analytical framework for unpacking the meaning of equity in human-AI dialogues. Using this framework, we conducted an auditing study to examine how GPT-3 responded to different sub-populations on crucial science and social topics: climate change and the Black Lives Matter (BLM) movement. Our corpus consists of over 20,000 rounds of dialogues between GPT-3 and 3290 individuals who vary in gender, race and ethnicity, education level, English as a first language, and opinions toward the issues. We found a substantively worse user experience with GPT-3 among the opinion and the education minority subpopulations; however, these two groups achieved the largest knowledge gain, changing attitudes toward supporting BLM and climate change efforts after the chat. We traced these user experience divides to conversational differences and found that GPT-3 used more negative expressions when it responded to the education and opinion minority groups, compared to its responses to the majority groups. We discuss the implications of our findings for a deliberative conversational AI system that centralizes diversity, equity, and inclusion.
summary: Autoregressive language models, which use deep learning to produce human-like texts, have become increasingly widespread. Such models are powering popular virtual assistants in areas like smart health, finance, and autonomous driving. While the parameters of these large language models are improving, concerns persist that these models might not work equally for all subgroups in society. Despite growing discussions of AI fairness across disciplines, there lacks systemic metrics to assess what equity means in dialogue systems and how to engage different populations in the assessment loop. Grounded in theories of deliberative democracy and science and technology studies, this paper proposes an analytical framework for unpacking the meaning of equity in human-AI dialogues. Using this framework, we conducted an auditing study to examine how GPT-3 responded to different sub-populations on crucial science and social topics: climate change and the Black Lives Matter (BLM) movement. Our corpus consists of over 20,000 rounds of dialogues between GPT-3 and 3290 individuals who vary in gender, race and ethnicity, education level, English as a first language, and opinions toward the issues. We found a substantively worse user experience with GPT-3 among the opinion and the education minority subpopulations; however, these two groups achieved the largest knowledge gain, changing attitudes toward supporting BLM and climate change efforts after the chat. We traced these user experience divides to conversational differences and found that GPT-3 used more negative expressions when it responded to the education and opinion minority groups, compared to its responses to the majority groups. We discuss the implications of our findings for a deliberative conversational AI system that centralizes diversity, equity, and inclusion.
abstract_zh: 自回归语言模型利用深度学习来生成类似人类的文本，已经变得越来越普遍。此类模型为智能健康、金融和自动驾驶等领域的流行虚拟助手提供支持。虽然这些大型语言模型的参数正在改进，但人们仍然担心这些模型可能无法平等地适用于社会中的所有亚群体。尽管跨学科对人工智能公平性的讨论越来越多，但缺乏系统性指标来评估公平性在对话系统中的含义以及如何让不同人群参与评估循环。本文以协商民主和科学技术研究理论为基础，提出了一个分析框架，用于解读人类与人工智能对话中公平的含义。利用这个框架，我们进行了一项审计研究，以考察 GPT-3 如何在关键科学和社会主题上对不同人群做出反应：气候变化和黑人生命也是命 (BLM) 运动。我们的语料库由 GPT-3 和 3290 名个人之间的 20,000 多轮对话组成，这些人的性别、种族和民族、教育水平、英语作为第一语言以及对问题的看法各不相同。我们发现，在意见和教育少数群体中，GPT-3 的用户体验明显较差；然而，这两个群体获得了最大的知识收获，在聊天后改变了支持 BLM 和气候变化努力的态度。我们将这些用户体验差异追溯到对话差异，发现与对多数群体的回应相比，GPT-3 在回应教育和意见少数群体时使用了更多负面表达。我们讨论了我们的研究结果对集中多样性、公平性和包容性的审慎对话人工智能系统的影响。

AI.tldr: 本文研究GPT-3在气候变化和BLM运动议题上对不同社会群体的回应差异，评估对话式AI的公平性。
AI.motivation: 现有AI公平性研究缺乏系统性评估对话系统公平性的指标，且不同群体在评估中的参与不足。
AI.method: 基于协商民主理论和科技研究框架，通过审计研究分析20,000轮对话数据，比较不同人口统计学和社会观点群体的体验差异。
AI.result: 教育和观点少数群体体验更差但知识获取最多，GPT-3对其使用更多负面表达，导致体验差距。
AI.conclusion: 对话式AI需整合多样性、公平性和包容性，以改善用户体验并促进社会议题讨论。

authors_text: Seoyoung Ahn,Hossein Adeli,Gregory J. Zelinsky
title: Reconstruction-guided attention improves the robustness and shape processing of neural networks
title_zh: 重建引导的注意力提高了神经网络的鲁棒性和形状处理
comment: paper accepted to SVRHM, Neurips workshop 2022
comments: paper accepted to SVRHM, Neurips workshop 2022
abstract: Many visual phenomena suggest that humans use top-down generative or reconstructive processes to create visual percepts (e.g., imagery, object completion, pareidolia), but little is known about the role reconstruction plays in robust object recognition. We built an iterative encoder-decoder network that generates an object reconstruction and used it as top-down attentional feedback to route the most relevant spatial and feature information to feed-forward object recognition processes. We tested this model using the challenging out-of-distribution digit recognition dataset, MNIST-C, where 15 different types of transformation and corruption are applied to handwritten digit images. Our model showed strong generalization performance against various image perturbations, on average outperforming all other models including feedforward CNNs and adversarially trained networks. Our model is particularly robust to blur, noise, and occlusion corruptions, where shape perception plays an important role. Ablation studies further reveal two complementary roles of spatial and feature-based attention in robust object recognition, with the former largely consistent with spatial masking benefits in the attention literature (the reconstruction serves as a mask) and the latter mainly contributing to the model's inference speed (i.e., number of time steps to reach a certain confidence threshold) by reducing the space of possible object hypotheses. We also observed that the model sometimes hallucinates a non-existing pattern out of noise, leading to highly interpretable human-like errors. Our study shows that modeling reconstruction-based feedback endows AI systems with a powerful attention mechanism, which can help us understand the role of generating perception in human visual processing.
summary: Many visual phenomena suggest that humans use top-down generative or reconstructive processes to create visual percepts (e.g., imagery, object completion, pareidolia), but little is known about the role reconstruction plays in robust object recognition. We built an iterative encoder-decoder network that generates an object reconstruction and used it as top-down attentional feedback to route the most relevant spatial and feature information to feed-forward object recognition processes. We tested this model using the challenging out-of-distribution digit recognition dataset, MNIST-C, where 15 different types of transformation and corruption are applied to handwritten digit images. Our model showed strong generalization performance against various image perturbations, on average outperforming all other models including feedforward CNNs and adversarially trained networks. Our model is particularly robust to blur, noise, and occlusion corruptions, where shape perception plays an important role. Ablation studies further reveal two complementary roles of spatial and feature-based attention in robust object recognition, with the former largely consistent with spatial masking benefits in the attention literature (the reconstruction serves as a mask) and the latter mainly contributing to the model's inference speed (i.e., number of time steps to reach a certain confidence threshold) by reducing the space of possible object hypotheses. We also observed that the model sometimes hallucinates a non-existing pattern out of noise, leading to highly interpretable human-like errors. Our study shows that modeling reconstruction-based feedback endows AI systems with a powerful attention mechanism, which can help us understand the role of generating perception in human visual processing.
abstract_zh: 许多视觉现象表明，人类使用自上而下的生成或重建过程来创建视觉感知（例如，图像、对象完成、幻想性视错觉），但人们对重建在鲁棒对象识别中的作用知之甚少。我们构建了一个迭代编码器-解码器网络，该网络生成对象重建并将其用作自上而下的注意力反馈，以路由最相关的空间和特征信息来前馈对象识别过程。我们使用具有挑战性的分布外数字识别数据集 MNIST-C 测试了该模型，其中 15 种不同类型的转换和损坏应用于手写数字图像。我们的模型对各种图像扰动表现出强大的泛化性能，平均优于所有其他模型，包括前馈 CNN 和对抗训练网络。我们的模型对于模糊、噪声和遮挡损坏特别稳健，其中形状感知起着重要作用。消融研究进一步揭示了空间注意力和基于特征的注意力在鲁棒目标识别中的两个互补作用，前者在很大程度上与注意力文献中的空间掩蔽益处一致（重建充当掩模），而后者主要通过减少可能的物体假设的空间来提高模型的推理速度（即达到某个置信阈值的时间步数）。我们还观察到，该模型有时会从噪声中产生一种不存在的模式，从而导致高度可解释的类似人类的错误。我们的研究表明，基于重建的反馈建模赋予人工智能系统强大的注意力机制，这可以帮助我们理解生成感知在人类视觉处理中的作用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于重建引导的注意力机制，以提升神经网络在形状处理和鲁棒性方面的性能。
AI.motivation: 人类视觉系统通过自上而下的生成过程实现鲁棒物体识别，但重建机制在其中的作用尚不明确。现有模型在面对图像扰动时表现不足，需探索更有效的注意力机制。
AI.method: 构建迭代编码-解码网络，生成物体重建作为自上而下注意力反馈，引导前馈网络聚焦关键空间与特征信息。
AI.result: 在MNIST-C数据集上，模型平均性能优于CNN和对抗训练网络，尤其对模糊、噪声和遮挡具有强鲁棒性；消融实验揭示空间注意力与特征注意力的互补作用。
AI.conclusion: 重建反馈机制赋予AI类人注意力能力，为理解人类视觉生成感知的作用提供新视角，同时提升模型在复杂场景下的鲁棒性。

authors_text: Luciano Vinas,Arash A. Amini,Jade Fischer,Atchar Sudhyadhom
title: LapGM: A Multisequence MR Bias Correction and Normalization Model
title_zh: LapGM：多序列 MR 偏差校正和归一化模型
abstract: A spatially regularized Gaussian mixture model, LapGM, is proposed for the bias field correction and magnetic resonance normalization problem. The proposed spatial regularizer gives practitioners fine-tuned control between balancing bias field removal and preserving image contrast preservation for multi-sequence, magnetic resonance images. The fitted Gaussian parameters of LapGM serve as control values which can be used to normalize image intensities across different patient scans. LapGM is compared to well-known debiasing algorithm N4ITK in both the single and multi-sequence setting. As a normalization procedure, LapGM is compared to known techniques such as: max normalization, Z-score normalization, and a water-masked region-of-interest normalization. Lastly a CUDA-accelerated Python package $\texttt{lapgm}$ is provided from the authors for use.
summary: A spatially regularized Gaussian mixture model, LapGM, is proposed for the bias field correction and magnetic resonance normalization problem. The proposed spatial regularizer gives practitioners fine-tuned control between balancing bias field removal and preserving image contrast preservation for multi-sequence, magnetic resonance images. The fitted Gaussian parameters of LapGM serve as control values which can be used to normalize image intensities across different patient scans. LapGM is compared to well-known debiasing algorithm N4ITK in both the single and multi-sequence setting. As a normalization procedure, LapGM is compared to known techniques such as: max normalization, Z-score normalization, and a water-masked region-of-interest normalization. Lastly a CUDA-accelerated Python package $\texttt{lapgm}$ is provided from the authors for use.
abstract_zh: 针对偏置场校正和磁共振归一化问题，提出了空间正则化高斯混合模型LapGM。所提出的空间正则化器为从业者提供了平衡偏置场去除和保留多序列磁共振图像的图像对比度之间的微调控制。 LapGM 的拟合高斯参数用作控制值，可用于标准化不同患者扫描的图像强度。在单序列和多序列设置中，LapGM 与著名的去偏算法 N4ITK 进行了比较。作为标准化过程，LapGM 与已知技术进行比较，例如：最大标准化、Z 分数标准化和水掩模感兴趣区域标准化。最后作者提供了一个CUDA加速的Python包$\texttt{lapgm}$供使用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为LapGM的多序列磁共振（MR）图像偏置校正和归一化模型，通过空间正则化高斯混合模型实现图像强度归一化。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理多序列MR图像时，难以在去除偏置场与保持图像对比度之间取得平衡，且缺乏针对多序列数据的统一归一化方案。
AI.method: 基于空间正则化高斯混合模型（LapGM），通过引入空间正则化项调节偏置校正与对比度保留的权衡，并利用拟合的高斯参数实现跨患者扫描的强度归一化。
AI.result: LapGM在单序列和多序列场景下均优于经典去偏算法N4ITK，在归一化效果上超越最大值归一化、Z-score归一化等传统方法。
AI.conclusion: LapGM为多序列MR图像提供了更精确的偏置校正与归一化方案，并通过CUDA加速的Python工具包实现方法落地。

authors_text: Shivam Vats,Maxim Likhachev,Oliver Kroemer
title: Efficient Recovery Learning using Model Predictive Meta-Reasoning
title_zh: 使用模型预测元推理进行高效恢复学习
comment: To appear in the International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2023
comments: To appear in the International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA) 2023
abstract: Operating under real world conditions is challenging due to the possibility of a wide range of failures induced by execution errors and state uncertainty. In relatively benign settings, such failures can be overcome by retrying or executing one of a small number of hand-engineered recovery strategies. By contrast, contact-rich sequential manipulation tasks, like opening doors and assembling furniture, are not amenable to exhaustive hand-engineering. To address this issue, we present a general approach for robustifying manipulation strategies in a sample-efficient manner. Our approach incrementally improves robustness by first discovering the failure modes of the current strategy via exploration in simulation and then learning additional recovery skills to handle these failures. To ensure efficient learning, we propose an online algorithm called Meta-Reasoning for Skill Learning (MetaReSkill) that monitors the progress of all recovery policies during training and allocates training resources to recoveries that are likely to improve the task performance the most. We use our approach to learn recovery skills for door-opening and evaluate them both in simulation and on a real robot with little fine-tuning. Compared to open-loop execution, our experiments show that even a limited amount of recovery learning improves task success substantially from 71% to 92.4% in simulation and from 75% to 90% on a real robot.
summary: Operating under real world conditions is challenging due to the possibility of a wide range of failures induced by execution errors and state uncertainty. In relatively benign settings, such failures can be overcome by retrying or executing one of a small number of hand-engineered recovery strategies. By contrast, contact-rich sequential manipulation tasks, like opening doors and assembling furniture, are not amenable to exhaustive hand-engineering. To address this issue, we present a general approach for robustifying manipulation strategies in a sample-efficient manner. Our approach incrementally improves robustness by first discovering the failure modes of the current strategy via exploration in simulation and then learning additional recovery skills to handle these failures. To ensure efficient learning, we propose an online algorithm called Meta-Reasoning for Skill Learning (MetaReSkill) that monitors the progress of all recovery policies during training and allocates training resources to recoveries that are likely to improve the task performance the most. We use our approach to learn recovery skills for door-opening and evaluate them both in simulation and on a real robot with little fine-tuning. Compared to open-loop execution, our experiments show that even a limited amount of recovery learning improves task success substantially from 71% to 92.4% in simulation and from 75% to 90% on a real robot.
abstract_zh: 由于执行错误和状态不确定性可能引发各种故障，因此在现实条件下运行具有挑战性。在相对良性的设置中，可以通过重试或执行少量手工设计的恢复策略之一来克服此类故障。相比之下，接触丰富的顺序操作任务，例如开门和组装家具，不适合详尽的手工设计。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种以样本有效的方式强化操纵策略的通用方法。我们的方法首先通过模拟探索发现当前策略的故障模式，然后学习额外的恢复技能来处理这些故障，从而逐步提高鲁棒性。为了确保高效的学习，我们提出了一种称为技能学习元推理（MetaReSkill）的在线算法，该算法可以监控训练期间所有恢复策略的进度，并将训练资源分配给最有可能提高任务性能的恢复。我们使用我们的方法来学习开门的恢复技能，并在模拟和几乎没有微调的真实机器人上对其进行评估。与开环执行相比，我们的实验表明，即使是有限数量的恢复学习，也可以将模拟中的任务成功率从 71% 大幅提高到 92.4%，在真实机器人中从 75% 提高到 90%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种高效恢复学习方法，通过模拟探索与元推理算法提升复杂操作任务的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 传统手动设计的恢复策略难以应对复杂连续操作任务中的各种失败模式，需更高效的自动化解决方案。
AI.method: 通过模拟探索发现当前策略的失败模式，学习额外恢复技能，并设计MetaReSkill算法动态分配训练资源。
AI.result: 在模拟环境和真实机器人上，任务成功率分别从71%提升至92.4%和75%提升至90%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能有效提升复杂操作任务的鲁棒性，少量恢复学习即可显著提高成功率。

search_text: Efficient Recovery Learning using Model Predictive Meta-Reasoning 使用模型预测元推理进行高效恢复学习 Operating under real world conditions is challenging due to the possibility of a wide range of failures induced by execution errors and state uncertainty. In relatively benign settings, such failures can be overcome by retrying or executing one of a small number of hand-engineered recovery strategies. By contrast, contact-rich sequential manipulation tasks, like opening doors and assembling furniture, are not amenable to exhaustive hand-engineering. To address this issue, we present a general approach for robustifying manipulation strategies in a sample-efficient manner. Our approach incrementally improves robustness by first discovering the failure modes of the current strategy via exploration in simulation and then learning additional recovery skills to handle these failures. To ensure efficient learning, we propose an online algorithm called Meta-Reasoning for Skill Learning (MetaReSkill) that monitors the progress of all recovery policies during training and allocates training resources to recoveries that are likely to improve the task performance the most. We use our approach to learn recovery skills for door-opening and evaluate them both in simulation and on a real robot with little fine-tuning. Compared to open-loop execution, our experiments show that even a limited amount of recovery learning improves task success substantially from 71% to 92.4% in simulation and from 75% to 90% on a real robot. 由于执行错误和状态不确定性可能引发各种故障，因此在现实条件下运行具有挑战性。在相对良性的设置中，可以通过重试或执行少量手工设计的恢复策略之一来克服此类故障。相比之下，接触丰富的顺序操作任务，例如开门和组装家具，不适合详尽的手工设计。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种以样本有效的方式强化操纵策略的通用方法。我们的方法首先通过模拟探索发现当前策略的故障模式，然后学习额外的恢复技能来处理这些故障，从而逐步提高鲁棒性。为了确保高效的学习，我们提出了一种称为技能学习元推理（MetaReSkill）的在线算法，该算法可以监控训练期间所有恢复策略的进度，并将训练资源分配给最有可能提高任务性能的恢复。我们使用我们的方法来学习开门的恢复技能，并在模拟和几乎没有微调的真实机器人上对其进行评估。与开环执行相比，我们的实验表明，即使是有限数量的恢复学习，也可以将模拟中的任务成功率从 71% 大幅提高到 92.4%，在真实机器人中从 75% 提高到 90%。 本文提出一种高效恢复学习方法，通过模拟探索与元推理算法提升复杂操作任务的鲁棒性。 Shivam Vats,Maxim Likhachev,Oliver Kroemer cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Jeremy Ocampo,Matthew A. Price,Jason D. McEwen
title: Scalable and Equivariant Spherical CNNs by Discrete-Continuous (DISCO) Convolutions
title_zh: 通过离散连续 (DISCO) 卷积实现可扩展且等变的球形 CNN
abstract: No existing spherical convolutional neural network (CNN) framework is both computationally scalable and rotationally equivariant. Continuous approaches capture rotational equivariance but are often prohibitively computationally demanding. Discrete approaches offer more favorable computational performance but at the cost of equivariance. We develop a hybrid discrete-continuous (DISCO) group convolution that is simultaneously equivariant and computationally scalable to high-resolution. While our framework can be applied to any compact group, we specialize to the sphere. Our DISCO spherical convolutions exhibit $\text{SO}(3)$ rotational equivariance, where $\text{SO}(n)$ is the special orthogonal group representing rotations in $n$-dimensions. When restricting rotations of the convolution to the quotient space $\text{SO}(3)/\text{SO}(2)$ for further computational enhancements, we recover a form of asymptotic $\text{SO}(3)$ rotational equivariance. Through a sparse tensor implementation we achieve linear scaling in number of pixels on the sphere for both computational cost and memory usage. For 4k spherical images we realize a saving of $10^9$ in computational cost and $10^4$ in memory usage when compared to the most efficient alternative equivariant spherical convolution. We apply the DISCO spherical CNN framework to a number of benchmark dense-prediction problems on the sphere, such as semantic segmentation and depth estimation, on all of which we achieve the state-of-the-art performance.
summary: No existing spherical convolutional neural network (CNN) framework is both computationally scalable and rotationally equivariant. Continuous approaches capture rotational equivariance but are often prohibitively computationally demanding. Discrete approaches offer more favorable computational performance but at the cost of equivariance. We develop a hybrid discrete-continuous (DISCO) group convolution that is simultaneously equivariant and computationally scalable to high-resolution. While our framework can be applied to any compact group, we specialize to the sphere. Our DISCO spherical convolutions exhibit $\text{SO}(3)$ rotational equivariance, where $\text{SO}(n)$ is the special orthogonal group representing rotations in $n$-dimensions. When restricting rotations of the convolution to the quotient space $\text{SO}(3)/\text{SO}(2)$ for further computational enhancements, we recover a form of asymptotic $\text{SO}(3)$ rotational equivariance. Through a sparse tensor implementation we achieve linear scaling in number of pixels on the sphere for both computational cost and memory usage. For 4k spherical images we realize a saving of $10^9$ in computational cost and $10^4$ in memory usage when compared to the most efficient alternative equivariant spherical convolution. We apply the DISCO spherical CNN framework to a number of benchmark dense-prediction problems on the sphere, such as semantic segmentation and depth estimation, on all of which we achieve the state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 现有的球形卷积神经网络 (CNN) 框架既不具有计算可扩展性，又具有旋转等变性。连续方法捕获旋转等方差，但通常计算要求过高。离散方法提供了更有利的计算性能，但以等方差为代价。我们开发了一种混合离散连续（DISCO）组卷积，它同时是等变的并且在计算上可扩展至高分辨率。虽然我们的框架可以应用于任何紧凑的群体，但我们专注于该领域。我们的 DISCO 球形卷积表现出 $\text{SO}(3)$ 旋转等变性，其中 $\text{SO}(n)$ 是表示 $n$ 维度旋转的特殊正交群。当将卷积的旋转限制在商空间 $\text{SO}(3)/\text{SO}(2)$ 以进一步增强计算时，我们恢复了渐近 $\text{SO}(3)$ 旋转等方差的形式。通过稀疏张量实现，我们在计算成本和内存使用方面实现了球体上像素数量的线性缩放。对于 4k 球面图像，与最有效的替代等变球面卷积相比，我们实现了计算成本节省 10^9 美元，内存使用节省 10^4 美元。我们将 DISCO 球形 CNN 框架应用于球体上的许多基准密集预测问题，例如语义分割和深度估计，在所有这些问题上我们都实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种混合离散-连续（DISCO）卷积方法，用于构建既计算可扩展又具有旋转等变性的球面卷积神经网络（CNN）。
AI.motivation: 现有球面CNN框架无法同时满足计算可扩展性和旋转等变性需求，连续方法计算成本高，离散方法则牺牲等变性。
AI.method: 开发离散-连续（DISCO）群卷积，结合离散与连续方法的优势，在球面场景下实现SO(3)旋转等变性，并通过稀疏张量实现线性计算与内存扩展。
AI.result: 在4k分辨率球面图像上，计算成本降低10^9次操作，内存减少10^4倍，且在语义分割和深度估计等任务中达到最先进性能。
AI.conclusion: DISCO框架有效平衡了计算效率与旋转等变性，为高分辨率球面CNN提供了可行解决方案，并在多个基准任务中验证了优越性。

authors_text: Sergey Bereg,José-Miguel Díaz-Báñez,Nadine Kroher,Inmaculada Ventura
title: Computing Melodic Templates in Oral Music Traditions
title_zh: 计算口语音乐传统中的旋律模板
abstract: The term melodic template or skeleton refers to a basic melody which is subject to variation during a music performance. In many oral music tradition, these templates are implicitly passed throughout generations without ever being formalized in a score. In this work, we introduce a new geometric optimization problem, the spanning tube problem, to approximate a melodic template for a set of labeled performance transcriptions corresponding to an specific style in oral music traditions. Given a set of $n$ piecewise linear functions, we solve the problem of finding a continuous function, $f^*$, and a minimum value, $\varepsilon^*$, such that, the vertical segment of length $2\varepsilon^*$ centered at $(x,f^*(x))$ intersects at least $p$ functions ($p\leq n$). The method explored here also provide a novel tool for quantitatively assess the amount of melodic variation which occurs across performances.
summary: The term melodic template or skeleton refers to a basic melody which is subject to variation during a music performance. In many oral music tradition, these templates are implicitly passed throughout generations without ever being formalized in a score. In this work, we introduce a new geometric optimization problem, the spanning tube problem, to approximate a melodic template for a set of labeled performance transcriptions corresponding to an specific style in oral music traditions. Given a set of $n$ piecewise linear functions, we solve the problem of finding a continuous function, $f^*$, and a minimum value, $\varepsilon^*$, such that, the vertical segment of length $2\varepsilon^*$ centered at $(x,f^*(x))$ intersects at least $p$ functions ($p\leq n$). The method explored here also provide a novel tool for quantitatively assess the amount of melodic variation which occurs across performances.
abstract_zh: 术语旋律模板或骨架是指在音乐表演期间会发生变化的基本旋律。在许多口头音乐传统中，这些模板隐含地代代相传，从未在乐谱中正式化。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个新的几何优化问题，即跨管问题，来近似一组与口头音乐传统中的特定风格相对应的标记演奏转录的旋律模板。给定一组 $n$ 分段线性函数，我们解决寻找连续函数 $f^*$ 和最小值 $\varepsilon^*$ 的问题，使得以 $(x,f^*(x))$ 为中心、长度为 $2\varepsilon^*$ 的垂直线段与至少 $p$ 个函数相交 ($p\leq n$)。这里探索的方法还提供了一种新颖的工具，用于定量评估表演中发生的旋律变化量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种新方法，通过解决几何优化问题来近似口述音乐传统中的旋律模板，并量化不同表演间的旋律变化。
AI.motivation: 口述音乐传统的旋律模板通常未被正式记录，需开发工具以提取和量化其旋律变化规律。
AI.method: 引入几何优化问题'spanning tube问题'，通过寻找连续函数与误差范围，使垂直线段覆盖足够多的表演数据点。
AI.result: 成功构建旋律模板近似方法，并提供评估旋律变化的定量工具。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为分析口述音乐传统中的旋律结构与变异提供了有效途径。

conclusion: 该方法为分析口述音乐传统中的旋律结构与变异提供了有效途径。
search_text: Computing Melodic Templates in Oral Music Traditions 计算口语音乐传统中的旋律模板 The term melodic template or skeleton refers to a basic melody which is subject to variation during a music performance. In many oral music tradition, these templates are implicitly passed throughout generations without ever being formalized in a score. In this work, we introduce a new geometric optimization problem, the spanning tube problem, to approximate a melodic template for a set of labeled performance transcriptions corresponding to an specific style in oral music traditions. Given a set of $n$ piecewise linear functions, we solve the problem of finding a continuous function, $f^*$, and a minimum value, $\varepsilon^*$, such that, the vertical segment of length $2\varepsilon^*$ centered at $(x,f^*(x))$ intersects at least $p$ functions ($p\leq n$). The method explored here also provide a novel tool for quantitatively assess the amount of melodic variation which occurs across performances. 术语旋律模板或骨架是指在音乐表演期间会发生变化的基本旋律。在许多口头音乐传统中，这些模板隐含地代代相传，从未在乐谱中正式化。在这项工作中，我们引入了一个新的几何优化问题，即跨管问题，来近似一组与口头音乐传统中的特定风格相对应的标记演奏转录的旋律模板。给定一组 $n$ 分段线性函数，我们解决寻找连续函数 $f^*$ 和最小值 $\varepsilon^*$ 的问题，使得以 $(x,f^*(x))$ 为中心、长度为 $2\varepsilon^*$ 的垂直线段与至少 $p$ 个函数相交 ($p\leq n$)。这里探索的方法还提供了一种新颖的工具，用于定量评估表演中发生的旋律变化量。 本文提出一种新方法，通过解决几何优化问题来近似口述音乐传统中的旋律模板，并量化不同表演间的旋律变化。 Sergey Bereg,José-Miguel Díaz-Báñez,Nadine Kroher,Inmaculada Ventura cs.SD,cs.IR,eess.AS

authors_text: Juan Miguel Valverde,Artem Shatillo,Jussi Tohka
title: Sauron U-Net: Simple automated redundancy elimination in medical image segmentation via filter pruning
title_zh: Sauron U-Net：通过滤波器修剪在医学图像分割中简单自动消除冗余
abstract: We introduce Sauron, a filter pruning method that eliminates redundant feature maps of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Sauron optimizes, jointly with the loss function, a regularization term that promotes feature maps clustering at each convolutional layer by reducing the distance between feature maps. Sauron then eliminates the filters corresponding to the redundant feature maps by using automatically adjusted layer-specific thresholds. Unlike most filter pruning methods, Sauron requires minimal changes to typical neural network optimization because it prunes and optimizes CNNs jointly, which, in turn, accelerates the optimization over time. Moreover, unlike with other cluster-based approaches, the user does not need to specify the number of clusters in advance, a hyperparameter that is difficult to tune. We evaluated Sauron and five state-of-the-art filter pruning methods on four medical image segmentation tasks. This is an area where little attention has been paid to filter pruning, but where smaller CNN models are desirable for local deployment, mitigating privacy concerns associated with cloud-based solutions. Sauron was the only method that achieved a reduction in model size of over 90% without deteriorating substantially the performance. Sauron also achieved, overall, the fastest models at inference time in machines with and without GPUs. Finally, we show through experiments that the feature maps of models pruned with Sauron are highly interpretable, which is essential for medical image segmentation.
summary: We introduce Sauron, a filter pruning method that eliminates redundant feature maps of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). Sauron optimizes, jointly with the loss function, a regularization term that promotes feature maps clustering at each convolutional layer by reducing the distance between feature maps. Sauron then eliminates the filters corresponding to the redundant feature maps by using automatically adjusted layer-specific thresholds. Unlike most filter pruning methods, Sauron requires minimal changes to typical neural network optimization because it prunes and optimizes CNNs jointly, which, in turn, accelerates the optimization over time. Moreover, unlike with other cluster-based approaches, the user does not need to specify the number of clusters in advance, a hyperparameter that is difficult to tune. We evaluated Sauron and five state-of-the-art filter pruning methods on four medical image segmentation tasks. This is an area where little attention has been paid to filter pruning, but where smaller CNN models are desirable for local deployment, mitigating privacy concerns associated with cloud-based solutions. Sauron was the only method that achieved a reduction in model size of over 90% without deteriorating substantially the performance. Sauron also achieved, overall, the fastest models at inference time in machines with and without GPUs. Finally, we show through experiments that the feature maps of models pruned with Sauron are highly interpretable, which is essential for medical image segmentation.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了 Sauron，一种过滤器修剪方法，可以消除卷积神经网络（CNN）的冗余特征图。 Sauron 与损失函数一起优化正则化项，该项通过减少特征图之间的距离来促进每个卷积层的特征图聚类。然后，Sauron 通过使用自动调整的特定于层的阈值来消除与冗余特征图对应的过滤器。与大多数过滤器修剪方法不同，Sauron 需要对典型神经网络优化进行最小的更改，因为它联合修剪和优化 CNN，从而随着时间的推移加速优化。此外，与其他基于集群的方法不同，用户不需要提前指定集群的数量，这是一个难以调整的超参数。我们在四个医学图像分割任务上评估了 Sauron 和五种最先进的滤波器修剪方法。这是一个很少关注过滤器修剪的领域，但较小的 CNN 模型适合本地部署，从而减轻与基于云的解决方案相关的隐私问题。 Sauron 是唯一能够将模型尺寸减小 90% 以上且性能没有大幅下降的方法。总体而言，Sauron 在有或没有 GPU 的机器上都实现了推理时间最快的模型。最后，我们通过实验证明，用 Sauron 修剪的模型的特征图具有高度可解释性，这对于医学图像分割至关重要。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Sauron方法，通过过滤器剪枝在医学图像分割中自动消除冗余特征图，实现模型压缩。
AI.motivation: 医学图像分割需要小型化模型以支持本地部署并解决隐私问题，但现有过滤器剪枝方法存在参数调节复杂或性能下降的问题。
AI.method: 联合优化损失函数与正则化项，通过特征图聚类减少冗余，并自动调整层阈值剪枝过滤器，无需预设聚类数量。
AI.result: 在四类医学分割任务中，Sauron实现超90%模型压缩且性能无明显下降，推理速度最快，且剪枝后模型特征图可解释性高。
AI.conclusion: Sauron为医学图像分割提供了高效、自动化的模型压缩方案，兼顾小型化、速度与可解释性，适用于本地部署场景。
